title: Gymnouratella pendula

Gymnema sylvestre is commonly used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and extracts.
taxonomy: Gymnouratella pendula is a species of diatom, and as such, it belongs to the kingdom Protista. It is classified within the phylum Bacillariophyta, which is a group of eukaryotic algae. The family to which Gymnouratella pendula belongs is likely to be part of the Naviculaceae family, given its characteristics as a type of diatom. The Naviculaceae family is a part of the order Naviculales, and this species is likely to be a member of this order due to its morphology. Further classification may be specific to the genus or species level, but the given classification is the most general known classification for Gymnouratella pendula.
